freshness_caveat: Coal data is 132 days old (as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark is 127 days old (as of 2025-12-31)—both are stale. Current merit-order slope and fuel-switch economics rely on more recent gas and renewable prices; coal valuation should be cross-checked against live thermal indices.
risk_flags: EU Storage at 11th percentile (34.4% full) vs. 5-yr seasonal norm—refill pace through summer will determine H2 power risk and gas call intensity.
Strait of Hormuz closure since late February sustains LNG export bottleneck; any reopening could rapidly deflate TTF premium and thermal margins.
Clean Spark at 92nd percentile (38.42 EUR/MWh) is historically elevated; coal at 8th percentile (96 USD/t, data 132 days old)—stale coal data masks true merit-order slope.
Renewable share collapsed 33% d/d to 18th percentile (27% of load); thermal dispatch will spike until wind/solar mean-reverts.
themes: Gas tight, storage depleted, LNG supply constrained by Strait closure
Clean Spark extended, coal weak—thermal generation in-the-money despite carbon at 29th percentile
Renewables at 18th percentile (27% share) lifting thermal call; GB premium widening on supply/demand tightness
top_drivers: Renewable collapse to 18th percentile (−33% d/d) forces thermal to swing dispatch; Clean Spark extended to 92nd percentile on gas-fired marginal cost anchoring.
EU Storage at 11th percentile (34.4% full, 12.5 pp below seasonal) confirms tight gas regime despite TTF in mid-range (57th percentile); Hormuz closure sustains LNG supply risk premium.
Coal weak (8th percentile) and stale data; Clean Dark at 50th percentile masks true dark spread—thermal economics rest on gas scarcity and carbon pass-through (EUA 29th pct at 32.01 EUR/t).
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 92nd percentile signals sustained thermal strength; EU storage 12.5 pp below seasonal average underpins tight gas outlook despite Hormuz-driven LNG risk premium.

watchlist.metric: Strait of Hormuz reopening / LNG supply normalization
watchlist.why: Any resolution in next 2–4 weeks would ease global LNG glut, cap TTF upside, and compress Clean Spark via lower gas marginal cost.

watchlist.metric: EU Storage refill trajectory (target 85% by Nov)
watchlist.why: Currently 12.5 pp below seasonal average at 34.4%; failure to refill on pace locks in Q3–Q4 power risk premium and keeps thermal fleet anchored to merit order.

watchlist.metric: US SPR drawdown pace and crude-LNG arb
watchlist.why: DOE released 17.5 Mbbl since March; accelerated SPR sales could further suppress crude and strengthen global LNG supply to EU, eroding TTF/power support.

watchlist.metric: Renewables mean reversion (27% to seasonal norm ~35–40%)
watchlist.why: 18th percentile renewable share drives sustained thermal call; any wind/solar improvement would flip merit order and compress spark/dark spreads.

watchlist.metric: GB–DE power spread (GB 111.78 EUR/MWh vs DE 138.0 EUR/MWh)
watchlist.why: GB premium of ~26 EUR/MWh reflects supply tightness and interconnector flow; widening premium signals cross-border constraint or GB-specific generation risk.
